name: Barkshack Gingermead
recipeIngredient: 3 oz Lemon Grass or other herb/spice
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil 1.5 gallons of water, then add the honey, corn sugar, grated ginger root, citric acid, and Irish moss. Mix until dissolved.
2. Turn off the heat. If adding fruit, strain out most of the grated ginger using a small strainer.
3. Add crushed fruit to the hot liquid (now called "must") and steep for 15-20 minutes.
4. Pour the entire contents into a 6-gallon plastic primary fermenter. Add cold water until the total volume reaches 5 1/2 gallons.
5. When the must cools to 70°F-78°F, rehydrate the yeast according to package directions and stir it into the fermenter (this is called pitching).
6. Cover with a lid and install an airlock.
7. After the specific gravity falls to 1.020 or after seven days, whichever comes first, transfer the brew to a secondary fermenter (5-gallon glass carboy) using a siphon.
8. If fruit was used, carefully strain out most of the fermented fruit before transferring to the secondary fermenter.
9. Install an airlock in the secondary fermenter and let it age for 1-1.5 months.
10. Boil water and corn sugar until dissolved (cool).
11. Transfer the cooled syrup to a 5-gallon bucket (bottling bucket) using a siphon, being careful not to splash.
12. Slightly stir the mead in the bucket before bottling.
13. If desired, add strained tea or herb/spice infusion at this stage.
14. Bottle the mead into returnable beer bottles. Cap securely and store away from light for up to a year. The flavor will develop over time, becoming smoother and less sharp with continued aging.
